{{Quote|Our good Master Spang at the ''Beach'' suggested starting with a fellow named Drake at one of the spaceport taverns, did he not?|[[Loh'khar]] the Finder and Captain Kaiya Adrimetrum discuss Spang's advice as they approach Pembric II|<ref name="Crisis (RPG adventure)">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Crisis (adventure)|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref>}}
'''Spang''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]]. He owned and operated [[Spang's]], a repair bay which was located on the [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Regarded as a genius engineer, Spang led the team hired by the [[Tenloss Syndicate/Legends|Tenloss Syndicate]] to produce the [[Hornet-class interceptor|''Hornet''-class interceptor]].

Spang's services modifying and repairing vessels were in great demand by the various individuals who frequented ''Tanquilla Beach'', and he had a waiting list several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] long. He was responsible for acquiring and installing ''Tanquilla Beach''{{'s}} defensive weaponry, and was a solid ally of [[Bequesh]], the [[Space station/Legends|station]] [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]]. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], his granddaughter, [[Fia (engineer)|Fia]], supervised the [[Engine/Legends|engine]] refit of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' with Spang's blessing as he was too busy to work on the vessel himself.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Sergeant Lofryyhn makes the…strongest possible recommendation that we lay over until we can have the ship's systems checked ma'am. He says that if the ''Beacher'' engineer was right, Pembric II is probably the best place to find the folks who installed this system for Sarne, or at least know something about it.|[[Petty Officer/Legends|Petty Officer]] [[Colton (FarStar crewmember)|Colton]] briefs Captain Adrimetrum on route to Pembric II|<ref name="Crisis (RPG adventure)" />}}
Spang, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] who owned and operated a repair bay on the [[Tanquilla Beach/Legends|''Tanquilla Beach'']] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The repair bay, known as [[Spang's]], was the largest on the [[Space station/Legends|station]], and was capable of modifying and repairing [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]]. His repair bay was regarded as the best on the station, and he was acknowledged as a master of his craft, known for his first rate work, ability to seemingly work miracles, and devise ingenious modifications. It was rumored that Spang had access to [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] Research and Development projects, but he never elaborated on such claims.<ref name="SD" />

Spang was an ally of [[Bequesh]], the [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] of ''Tanquilla Beach''; Spang had procured and installed the station's impressive defensive array of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors from Imperial [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]]. Over the [[Standard year/Legends|years]], many individuals had tried to oust Spang from his prime spot on the station. However, what many did not realize was that interfering with Spang drew Bequesh's attention, and he did not take kindly to anyone messing with Spang. Spang, for his part, had no need to justify his usefulness to ''Tanquilla Beach''; he invariably had a waiting list of ships several [[Standard month/Legends|months]] long waiting for his services.<ref name="SD" />

At some point, Spang was hired by the [[Tenloss Syndicate/Legends|Tenloss Syndicate]], a shadowy criminal organization. He led a freelance team made up of former Imperial engineers to produce a short-range [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] for the Syndicate. Spang and his team based the design on the mission profile of the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]], and succeeded in creating the [[Hornet-class interceptor|''Hornet''-class interceptor]], a fast, light and easily mass-produced craft.<ref name="JASB" />

Spang was also friends and drinking buddies with [[ConJob]], an [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] who doubled as a [[florist]]. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Spang's granddaughter, [[Fia (engineer)|Fia]], came to ''Tanquilla Beach'' to intern with him. Fia, a recent graduate of [[Corellia University/Legends|Corellia University's]] [[School of Starship Engineering]], was a natural and talented [[hypernautics]] engineer in her own right. He agreed to let her stay on the station, but was privately worried that her staying on the station was not a good idea.<ref name="SD" />

Months after Fia's arrival, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'' arrived at the shadowport seeking repairs to the vessel's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], which had been damaged by a combination of [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] and [[Skirmish at Shintel|combat]] in the nearby [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]]. Spang's repair bay was the only facility on the station capable of conducting the repairs, but as Spang was extremely busy, he was unable to assist the ''FarStar'', and even refused to meet with the command crew to discuss terms. However, his friend ConJob persuaded Spang to help them out—ConJob had lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Siluria III]] and his family had been [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the Imperial occupation. The current [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the ''FarStar'' was [[Kaiya Adrimetrum]], who had led the [[Siluria III resistance|resistance]] against the Empire. Spang agreed to let Fia oversee the repairs and refit for a fee of 50,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="SD" />

Fia overhauled the ''FarStar''{{'s}} [[Engine/Legends|engines]], and found evidence of programming trapdoors embedded in the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette's]] systems which had allowed the vessel to be sabotaged at [[Shintel]]. The trapdoors had been put there by Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the former owner of the vessel. Sarne had intended the corvette to perform special missions for him, and took precautions to safeguard the vessel in case a commanding [[Officer/Legends|officer]] attempted to steal the ship or subvert Sarne's authority. Fia was confident that she could remove the programming trapdoors in the engineering systems, but admitted that she did not have enough [[Technician/Legends|technical]] knowledge to search the rest of the ship for more. Spang, while stopping in to see how Fia was coping, noted that the work was similar to some he had seen on [[Pembric II]]. He advised [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Adrimetrum that if she wanted to avoid any more trouble with booby trapped circuitry, then she should travel to Pembric II and find [[Breslin Drake]]. Drake had contacts all over Pembric II, and would be able to find someone to help her solve the technical problems on the ship.<ref name="SD" />

==Personality and traits==
Spang was a short, old man with a cheerful disposition and a beaming smile. He was extremely protective of his granddaughter, Fia, and worried that ''Tanquilla Beach'' was not the right place for her to work in. An engineering genius, Spang was in high demand by the patrons of ''Tanquilla Beach'' to repair and modify their vessels, and had a tendency to get lost in his work. When the ''FarStar'' arrived at the station, Spang was so busy that he refused to see their representatives, and had to be convinced to help the New Republic vessel by his friend, ConJob. He was well acquainted with the running of ''Tanquilla Beach'' due to his association with Bequesh, and was fully capable of running a successful starship repair and modification business for many years. He had knowledge of several languages, and was able to determine the value of useful items.<ref name="SD" />

Spang was trained to fly capital ships and [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]], and could repair most types of starships, including large vessels, personal transports and [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. He could also repair starfighter weaponry, and program and repair [[Computer/Legends|computers]]. He was generally clad in greasy trousers and a [[shirt]], with a tool-filled workbelt around his waist. He carried a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="SD" />

==Behind the scenes==
Spang appeared in ''[[Shintel Downtime]]'', the fourth [[Roleplaying game|RPG adventure]] in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' by [[West End Games]] which was written by [[Paul Sudlow]] and published in [[1995]].<ref name="TDC">''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]''</ref> Spang charges the players 50,000 credits for the engine repair, although he can be talked down to 45,000 if the players are members of the [[Corellian Merchants' Guild/Legends|Corellian Merchants' Guild]].<ref name="SD" /> Spang's involvement with the creation of the ''Hornet''-class interceptor was revealed in ''[[The Jedi Academy Sourcebook]]'' in [[1996]],<ref name="JASB" /> and he was later namechecked in West End Games' ''[[Platt's Smugglers Guide]]'', published in [[1997]].<ref name="PSG">''[[Platt's Smugglers Guide]]''</ref>
